query: double exposure ghostly silhouette overlapping portrait
message: sub:double_exposure — anti_aesthetic — overlapping ghostly portraits, doubled silhouettes, layered composites

response.objective_caption: A black-and-white double portrait of a young girl with long light-colored hair standing in front of a white square tile wall. The girl faces the camera directly with a slight smile, her hair falling across her forehead. A second, fainter translucent image of the same girl appears superimposed above and behind her primary figure, creating a layered ghosted effect. The composition is in grayscale with visible grid lines of the tile pattern forming a regular geometric background.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A black-and-white double-exposure portrait of a young girl with long light-colored hair standing before a white square tile wall, featuring a soft, blurred ghost image of her face superimposed above her sharper primary figure. The surreal layered effect creates a dreamlike, uncanny atmosphere as the translucent second exposure appears to float behind her, with the tile grid visible through both layers. The intentional blur of the upper ghost image contrasts with the clarity of the main portrait, producing an otherworldly, subconscious quality that disrupts ordinary photographic realism.
